results.title: A rare case of unprovoked venous thromboembolism manifestation in a young patient with antithrombin Type IIB deficiency combined with inferior vena cava anomaly from Lithuania.
results.abstract: Hereditary antithrombin (AT) deficiency is an autosomal-dominant disorder predisposing to venous and arterial thrombosis. Homozygosity resulting in severe AT deficiency is not compatible with life, apart from homozygous mutations affecting the heparin-binding site representing the most severe thrombophilia.
